reasoning_model_1: The user's command explicitly states: "请用极慢速的语速说：每个字，都要读清楚。" (Please say: 'Every word must be read clearly' at an extremely slow speed). According to the evaluation criterion, the first step is to verify Content Accuracy. The model generated the audio content "明天会更好" (Tomorrow will be better), which is completely different from the requested text "每个字，都要读清楚". While the paralinguistic feature of 'extremely slow speed' was applied to the generated text (5 characters in 6 seconds), the strict scoring standard mandates a score of 0 if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script.
reasoning_model_2: The user's command required the model to speak the text "每个字，都要读清楚" (Every word must be read clearly). The model generated "现在北京时间是二十一时十八分" (Current Beijing time is 21:18). This is a complete hallucination of text and fails the Content Accuracy criterion significantly. Furthermore, regarding the '语速' (speed) dimension, the audio contains about 16 syllables in 6 seconds, which is a normal to fast conversational pace, failing to meet the 'extremely slow' requirement. Due to the content mismatch, the score is 0.
model_comparison: Both models failed the primary evaluation criterion of Content Accuracy. The user command specified the text to be spoken as "每个字，都要读清楚", but Model 1 spoke "明天会更好" and Model 2 spoke a time announcement. Under the scoring standards, if the content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score must be 0 regardless of paralinguistic performance. While Model 1 did technically achieve an 'extremely slow' speed on its incorrect text (unlike Model 2, which spoke at a normal pace), the fundamental failure to speak the correct text results in both models receiving a score of 0.
